RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: He won three times over 5f last August-October and is 8lb below his last winning mark; drops back in trip today but he's finished down the field on all five starts this season and has plenty to prove.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 6yo who is well handicapped on last season's best efforts for Michael Bell; however, it was a winless campaign and he failed to threaten at Yarmouth in April on his sole run this year; changed hands for 18,500gns last month and he needs to get back on track on this stable debut.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced on last two starts but hasn't run badly and he's 4lb lower than when runner-up over C&D (good) in June; capable of having a big say.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Merely sixth of eight at Goodwood last month but his two best performances have coincided with his two visits to Sandown, including a C&D win (good) in May; this lightly raced 3yo is firmly in calculations back here.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo who won his first three handicaps (6f, turf/AW) and bounced back from heavy Ascot defeat (6f, good) with respectable fourth at Kempton (6f, AW) latest; he has plenty of speed and this first crack at 5f is well worth a go.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Windsor (5f, good to firm) in May and second at Bath (5f, firm) next time; the track may not have suited at Brighton last time and he could bounce back with a big run.

RaceName: Tony Linnett Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Hold-up performer who won two in a row in May (5.7f/6f, good to firm) before keeping on for fifth of 13 at Windsor (5f, good to firm), and he was back on the scoresheet at Bath (5.7f, firm) three weeks ago; the return to a bare 5f may not be ideal but the stiff track compensates; respected.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Always behind on both starts; big outsider.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dubawi brother to 1m winner Blue Nazare (RPR 93); dam Listed-placed 6f winner (including 2yo; 96); the Charlie Appleby 2yos are in excellent form.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: From a top stable but hasn't achieved near enough in his two starts (well-held fourth latterly) to think he can win a novice.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 550,000euros son of Sea The Stars out of a 1m4f AW winner (RPR 94); stable 10-38 with 2yos this season at the time of writing; his pedigree points to longer trips suiting in due course but he'd need considering if the betting signals are strong.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 16-1 when never better than midfield on his 7f Lingfield (AW) debut 17 days ago; needs to have come on a lot.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150,000euros yearling who is the first foal of a winning half-sister to 1m4f Group 1 winner Shareta; gelded ahead of debut; the yard is going well in general but its 2yos have yet to really get going.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: This Ten Sovereigns colt cost 200,000gns at the breeze-ups and is a half-brother to winners UAE Prince (1m2f-1m4f; RPR 109) and Any Dream Will Do (1m; 86); in-form stable has a very healthy 2yo strike-rate this year, including a short-priced first-time-out winner in these silks at Nottingham last week; market confidence behind this one would look significant

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Stepped up on his debut and shaped quite well when a closing third in a C&D novice (good to firm) a fortnight ago; looks capable of better again and the clear pick of those with experience.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: American-bred colt who was green when quite a well-held fourth of ten on 7f Lingfield (AW) debut 17 days ago; more one for the longer term on that evidence.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £70,000 half-brother to four winners who is open to improvement on this second start; however, he was a well-beaten seventh of ten at 20-1 at Lingfield (7f, AW) this month and a sizeable step forward is needed.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late John Connolly
Spotlight: 80,000euros foal, 170,000gns yearling; seventh foal; Palace Pier half-brother to winners Treizieme (French 1m/8.5f including 2yo), Tik Tok Famous (US 7.5f/1m turf) and Goldis (Slovakian 1m2f); dam placed 1m1f in France, half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Elusive Kate; respected trainer yet to get going with his 2yos this season, but it will be no surprise to see a big run on debut.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; sixth foal; New Bay half-brother to winners Galiac (7f including 2yo; RPR 96), Gallic (1m 2yo/1m2f AW; 92), Galsworthy (1m4f AW; 82) and Galel (7f AW; 65); dam 1m3f/1m4f winner (including Listed; 106), out of 1m Group 1 winner Spinning Queen; may need this debut outing but is bred to be talented and is not ruled out.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 160,000euros yearling; first foal; dam made the frame over 6.5f-1m3f in France, half-sister to French winners Muette (1m4f Listed) and Sans Voix (1m 2yo Listed); trainer is enjoying an excellent season with his 2yos and this Sea The Stars newcomer could have a big say.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 95,000gns yearling; third foal; dam German 1m3f and 2m4f (Listed) winner (RPR 93), half-sister to French 1m4f Group 2 winner Now We Can, out of German 1m3f/1m4f winner; a market check is advised but this Ghaiyyath newcomer may come into his own over quite a bit further.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown some ability on both starts, when seventh of ten at Ascot (6f, good) then fifth of nine at Lingfield (7f, AW; 16-1 each time); however, takes on some interesting newcomers and could again prove vulnerable.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 700,000gns yearling; third foal; dam Listed-placed 10.5f/1m4f winner, sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Kingsbarns; it's likely his future lies over further but this Dubawi newcomer is nevertheless high on the list.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; Mehmas half-brother to 1m4f AW winner Rogue Impact (RPR 94); dam once-raced half-sister to winners Mukhadram (1m2f Group 1) and Woodland Aria (useful 7f/9.4f); with a leading stable; one to consider on debut.

RaceName: Martin Densham & Peter Deal Memorial British EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 72,000euros yearling; tenth foal; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Bay Of Plenty (RPR 97), half-sister to five winners including Glesga Gal (7f AW; 100) and Vivianite (1m; 99); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner; potential in pedigree and still early days but has to leave debut run miles behind.

RaceName: Doncaster Supports Autism In Racing EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: The Soldiers Of Fortune Iv & Partner
Spotlight: Fifth in two Flat maidens in Ireland (1m/1m4f) for Joseph O'Brien, showing fair form on the first occasion; no show in two recent hurdle starts since joining Olly Murphy and comes up against a couple of promising sorts back on the level.

RaceName: Doncaster Supports Autism In Racing EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Promise amidst inexperience in a useful Newbury maiden on debut and looked a useful prospect when easily taking a Ffos Las maiden (1m2f, good) under David Egan last month; surely more to come.

RaceName: Doncaster Supports Autism In Racing EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two AW starts last autumn, leaving his Kempton debut well behind when going down by only a neck at Southwell (1m); this is a slightly belated return but he should have more to offer for a leading stable going great guns; wears first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Doncaster Supports Autism In Racing EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Big prices and well held both starts.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won on debut at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) last month then finished a close third of 14 against males in the Listed Chesham Stakes at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm; led); leading claims back against fellow fillies.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Went very close on debut at Doncaster (6.5f, good to firm) last month before easily making all at 4-9 at Newbury (7f, good to firm); this is a tougher test of her credentials but she could be up to the task.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Improved upon her debut run to win at Carlisle (7f, good to firm) recently; had just a short head to spare and her best days are likely to be over further, but she's the type to keep on progressing and is not ruled out.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 11-2 when promising third of six on debut at Nottingham (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; up in grade today but the Nottingham form has received a firm boost from the runner-up and this 140,000euros breeze-up buy is open to considerable improvement; one to watch in the betting.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She's shown ability in defeat across her three starts (7f, AW/good to firm) but not enough to suggest she'll be making an impact this afternoon.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Built upon the promise of her debut third at Kempton (7f, AW) to win at Beverley (7.4f, good) last month; it was a small field last time and the return to a bare 7f isn't an obvious positive, but she's a likeable prospect for the stable that won this in 2019 and 2022.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Caught the eye when fifth to Aperoll on debut at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) in June before keeping on for respectable eighth in the Albany at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); runner-up in hot maiden at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last time and she might not be far away.

RaceName: European Bloodstock News EBF Star Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 2-1, rallied to make winning debut in three-runner novice at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) four weeks ago and the third has won since; this 230,000gns yearling is in top hands and could have lots more to offer, especially now upped to 7f; possible player.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Dark Angel colt who stepped up on turf debut when making all at Kempton (6f, AW) in May; freshened up since finishing a well held last of three in conditions race at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) later that month; blinkered first time now handicapping from a mark which isn't obviously generous.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Built on debut fourth when winning a 6f course novice (good to firm) 20 days ago; the form has been franked, including by the fourth making a winning nursery debut at Windsor on Monday; should be more to come.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth to Inner City Blues (also next-time-out winners in second/third) on 6f York debut and built on that, despite failing to land short odds, when close second over 7f at Ayr 17 days ago (both good ground); cheekpieces are added now making a quick switch to handicaps from a mark which looks workable.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't lived up to market expectations on all three starts but has steadily advanced her RPRs; didn't really sustain her effort when second on 7f Kempton nursery debut three weeks ago so the slight drop back in trip may help.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Well held on first two starts but showed a bit more when third of five to a Godolphin odds-on shot over 7f at Yarmouth (good to firm) three weeks ago; the first-time hood worn on that occasion is left off now dropping back a little in distance for her nursery debut.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in April and he produced one of his better turf runs when close fifth of eight at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last time; however, a record of 0-22 on grass doesn't inspire confidence.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced and well-bred 5yo by Frankel; won a novice at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in November 2024 for Roger Varian and then absent until Newmarket (1m, good to firm) a fortnight ago, when runner-up on his handicap and stable debut; could have a big say up in trip if avoiding the dreaded bounce.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who has failed to kick on since 2yo win for Andre Fabre in November 2024; well beaten at Yarmouth on last month's stable debut; others arrive with a lot less to prove.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Returned from a break with good third at Kempton (1m3f, AW) last month; his peak efforts have come on AW but he's lightly raced on turf, on which he's shown promise, and he could have a part to play.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form to win at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) this month and he's since been second at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm), albeit no match for the progressive winner; there's every chance he'll be in the mix.

RaceName: Sompo Insurance Handicap
Spotlight: Won on turf debut at Yarmouth (1m, good) in May before good second there (1m, good) seven days later, and she got up in the final strides at Salisbury (1m, good) last month; the step up in trip could be a positive for this lightly raced 4yo and there's lots to like.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't matched his fairly useful debut RPR for the Quinn yard in two subsequent outings but he did make quite an encouraging return from a year off for his new stable when fourth in C&D novice (good) ten weeks ago; potential improver in handicaps.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Half-brother to a Group 3 winner; justified market support when overcoming inexperience to win a 6f maiden at Lingfield (AW) in January but well below that level in two subsequent turf outings, returning from a gelding operation to finish a remote third of six in Leicester novice (good to firm) in a first-time tongue-tie (retained) 19 days ago; needs to leave that well behind now moving into a handicap.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win at the third attempt in 7f maiden at Southwell in April but has struggled in three handicaps since;

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: 5f maiden winner at Goodwood (good to firm) last June; reached the frame for the third time in handicaps this year when fourth of eight at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago but he'll need more to get his head back in front.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: All races at 6f, taking a step forward for the switch to turf when well on top from two subsequent winners in 6f Windsor maiden (good to firm) in May; his opening handicap mark looks manageable, particularly as he has the potential for better again.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Three handicap wins this year, the latest at Beverley (5f, good to soft) in May; creditable efforts in defeat in a trio of handicaps over this minimum trip since; just as effective at 6f and enters calculations with the Ivan Furtado stable among the winners.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Raced at 7f, winning Kempton maiden (AW) in January on fourth start; has run well to make the frame in her three handicaps since, latterly third of ten to Accademia (won since) here (good to firm) three weeks ago; led until inside the final furlong on that occasion so the drop to 6f is worth exploring.

RaceName: Summer Saturdays At Doncaster Handicap
Spotlight: Struck at the second time of asking in handicaps when making all under Lewis Chalkley at Ripon (6f, good) 17 days ago; his three closest pursuers haven't done much for the form since but he was comfortably on top and his unexposed profile provides the hope for better again.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Seven-time AW winner in Britain, has struggled in 16 starts between Flat and hurdles for current yard; pulled up on two hurdle starts; can't make a case for.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Five-race Flat maiden; struggled on stable debut in 2m handicap 55 days ago; makes hurdles debut; dam won over hurdles; needs switch of discipline to bring improvement.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved on bumper and maiden hurdle run on first start for this yard at this track 20 days ago when fourth of 14, albeit still beaten 20l; was keen and showed greenness that day so likely more to come dropped in trip; wears hood.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed promise when third in Clonmel bumper on debut in April; hasn't built on that since in three runs and up against it.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved on debut when making late gains to be beaten 17l into fourth at Ballinrobe (2m, gd) 58 days ago; type to progress but looked in need of further that day; good each-way claims.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan
Spotlight: Dam a bumper winner, half-sister to very smart dual-purpose performer Wicklow Brave; beaten 2-5 favourite on debut in Tramore bumper in August (form hasn't worked out); returned from break to be beaten 14l into fifth at Bellewstown (2m1f, gd) on hurdles debut 19 days ago, looked in need of further; will improve for that and a player.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Three starts over hurdles have been littered with jumping errors, albeit he ran okay at Gowran Park when eighth of 12; needs a big step forward after 269 days off.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran to a decent level last year, placed in three maiden hurdles over a range of trips on good ground; similar level when fourth at Listowel in May and shaped better than the bare result when beaten 12l into fifth at Kilbeggan 13 days ago; drops in trip, has experience edge and should go close

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Little to recommend based on two hurdle starts when three-figure prices; one for handicaps.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Big prices and ran accordingly in a bumper and maiden hurdle; difficult to see figuring.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Well-held in a Hexham bumper and Roscommon maiden hurdle, finished well behind Game Point in latter; best watched.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful Flat maiden, runner-up in four maidens and rated 77 in that sphere; placed in three maiden hurdles, latest at Roscommon (2m gd) 17 days ago when beaten 6.25l into third, shaping well as was unsuited by slow pace; should be winning one of these soon and the one to beat.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rated 65 on the Flat; pulled up in two hurdle runs, saddle slipped early lto; best watched.

RaceName: Kilmore Quay (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rated 40 on the Flat after seven runs; makes hurdles debut and needs significant improvement.

RaceName: George Lindon-Travers Memorial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won two of his three handicaps (on good) and beaten a head in the other (good to firm); collared the odds-on front-runner close home when upped to 1m6f in eight-runner race at York last time; raised another 5lb but progressive and still promising.

RaceName: George Lindon-Travers Memorial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: All four races on good ground; two places before she got off the mark in a four-runner maiden at Pontefract (1m2f) in June; favourite when upped to 1m4f for handicap debut at Epsom four weeks later, coming from off the pace for a creditable third of ten off this mark despite persistently hanging left; 1m6f is plausible on that display and she may still have more to give.

RaceName: George Lindon-Travers Memorial Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Improved when upped in trip for handicap debut at Lingfield (11.6f, good to firm) eight days ago, coming from last of three to lead 2f out and win by 12l under Alexandra Egan; has 6lb penalty (due to go up another 1lb) but did it easily that day and has clear potential for this sort of trip.

RaceName: Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
OwnerName: Noir Et Or Elevage S.A & Ecurie Skymarc Farm

RaceName: Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Prix Madame Jean Couturie (Listed Race) (Fillies) (Turf)
Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: It took him 17 attempts to put his head in front but has now won two of his last three, finding plenty tackling 7f for the first time when seeing off a couple of strong challengers in handicap at Thirsk (good to firm) 22 days ago; respected up 3lb.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Has run a number of good in defeat races this season, including fourth over C&D last month; went down only to a progressive 3yo at Newbury (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago and the third and fourth have franked that form; bold show likely.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Scored back-to-back over 7f at Catterick (good) in April and added a 6f success there (good again) in May; freshened up since finishing a below-par fourth there (6f again) eight weeks ago; out of the money on both visits to Doncaster, including three starts back.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Went close over C&D (good to firm) last month and 1m seemed to stretch him back here since (all wins at 7f, including here); his last success came off this mark; likely to be on the premises under Rodriguez.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up twice in May (C&D on good to firm first occasion); caught too far back when fourth over 1m here last time; likely still in good form but needs things to drop right the way he's ridden.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
OwnerName: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam
Spotlight: Won twice over 7f this time last year for Mick Appleby but has yet to get anywhere near for new yard this year; well handicapped but could only consider if the betting suggests he's likely to leave his recent efforts behind.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Opened this year's account when making all in amateur riders' race at Carlisle (6f, good to soft) last month; creditable fourth over 6f here next time but below par back at Carlisle 19 days ago; former C&D winner but has yet to defy a mark this high.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (7f, good) off 10lb higher last year; has largely struggled since but latest Catterick fourth from well off the pace was a step back in the right direction and has obvious handicapping scope if able to build on that.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Limerick bumper winner in Dec 2022; off the track for almost three years before return in March; hasn't shown that she retains ability in three runs; beaten 41l into fourth at Bellewstown 19 days ago; drops in trip.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful bumper performer, sole win coming at Punchestown (2m2f, y) in December, making all to beat Luciole Des Bordes by 1.75l; beaten 8l into sixth in Grade 2 Leopardstown contest in February; bit below form on last start; sets a decent standard if she translates her form to hurdles.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in bumper and maiden hurdle at Sligo (2m4f, gd) either side of below-par Listowel effort; run down by 105-rated Insouciant Dallier at Sligo; off 272 days and needs improvement but in top hands.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Went off 100-1 and ran accordingly on debut at Kilbeggan 13 days ago; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-6 in point-to-points but failed to complete on four occasions; well beaten on hurdles debut at Downpatrick nine days ago; best watched.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rated 96 after six runs in maiden hurdles; one of better efforts when beaten 6l into fifth over longer trip at Downpatrick 39 days ago; not out of it but needs improvement.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Two solid placed efforts in bumpers; hasn't matched that in three runs over hurdles but step in right direction when beaten 13.5l into fifth at Downpatrick (2m3f, gd) 40 days ago in first-time cheekpieces; needs another step forward and may want further.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved on moderate hurdling debut to finish third at Wexford (2m1f, gd) in August; backed that up with solid bumper run when second at Tipperary and continued good form when returning from break at Roscommon, beaten 5.5l into third; step up in trip likely to suit and big player.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister to smart 2m1f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Senecia; dam 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner; off the mark on third attempt in point-to-point 102 days ago; makes rules debut.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Huge price and beaten 31l on hurdles debut 39 days ago; unlikely to play a prominent role.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Dam 2m hurdle winner, sister to useful bumper winner Oakley Hall, beaten 19.5l into third in sole point-to-point start 116 days ago; makes rules debut.

RaceName: Curragh To Curragh Charity Cycle On August 29th Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten 31l on rules debut in bumper 17 days ago; needs to build signficantly on that on hurdles bow.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won off 4lb lower at Epsom (1m2f, good) last August in her second handicap; runner-up on next two starts but much less impact since (despite being prominent in the betting on last two outings) and needs to turn things around with the first-time tongue-tie.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win (ten races) was over 1m4f on the Kempton AW in April in first-time hood, which has been kept since; same mark today, having been out of form on last three outings (back to 1m2f for latest); others preferred.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Close third of six at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) on debut in May, which she's not lived up to in her two starts since but these are early days and this is her handicap debut; needs a second look.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; tailed off on her stable/handicap debut (1m4f, good to soft) early last month, but 33-1 winner at Chepstow (1m4f) next time and she followed up in four-runner race at same track (1m2f, again good to firm) eight days later; penalty makes her only another 2lb higher today and she's on a roll.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Sent handicapping for last three starts, comfortably on top at Bath (1m2f, good) on second occasion and making all in five-runner race at Salisbury (1m2f, good to firm) 12 days ago; hung on by a head last time and raised another 4lb today, which demands better but she has been on the upgrade.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Off 218 days when making her handicap debut in a first-time hood at Nottingham (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago, coming from towards rear to emerge in second of eight to an impressive winner; has the same mark today, could have come on for the run and gave hope for 1m2f.

RaceName: Weatherbys Racing Bank Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Improved when second of ten in a handicap at Windsor (upped to 1m2f, good to firm) early last month; fair fourth of seven at Bath one month later over 11.6f which might have stretched her (made most); may have more to offer back at this trip but it is needed.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced gelding who hasn't progressed from his 5f 2yo success, including in three outings between 7f and 1m for Ian Williams this year; this is a drop in grade but would need to see market support to consider.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: On a losing run but has reduced mark as a result and he was backed into joint favouritism at Pontefract last week only to blow the start; has a first-time tongue-tie added now; it'll be interesting to see whether his supporters are prepared to give him a second chance.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins off higher marks; has an inconsistent profile and hasn't built on April's Pontefract second in two subsequent outings but he might have just needed it at Brighton recently after three months off.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Latest Nottingham third in this headgear was a bit better and he'll be a threat to all if recapturing the form he showed when a close second over C&D (good to firm) off 2lb higher in April, particularly with Jack Callan taking an additional 3lb off.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 7f handicap at Redcar (good to firm) last summer off 2lb lower and bounced back to that sort of form when third of ten to Farandaway (runs in previous race) over 7f at Thirsk (good to firm) three weeks ago; in a lower grade now and should have a big say with Ivan Furtado among the winners.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
OwnerName: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Off the mark on first run over 1m at Leicester (good to firm) this month, despite not getting the best of runs; backed it up when running on from the rear for neck second over C&D (good to firm) last Saturday; out again before a 2lb rise for that kicks in.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Only win in 26 runs was a 1m nursery at York in October 2023 for Ralph Beckett; badly out of form this season and cheekpieces made no difference last time; they are replaced by a first-time tongue-tie now.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who has form figures of 12531 since wearing cheekpieces, most recently seeing off nine rivals at Carlisle (7f) in June; has a likeable profile for this grade but the wins have been achieved on good to soft/soft and she needs to prove she's as effective on today's likely much faster ground.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Took his time about getting off the mark but he did so in good style on Lingfield AW (7f) ten days ago and, although back up 9lb, he went close off this mark at Ascot last September; also has form over this C&D (one attempt); solid claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Minor form on first two runs for new yard but he contributed to their fine recent run when winning over C&D (first course run) 18 days ago; back up 3lb but he's still well treated on past form and should be capable of better on this surface now he's had some experience of it.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Both AW wins at Newcastle (1m and 7f) but he lost out only narrowly over C&D in March; has had ups and downs since but he came from well back when fourth of ten to Gunfighter over C&D recently; place chance.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: All four AW wins on Polytrack, the latest more than four years ago, and he's likely to need more than a respectable sixth at Newcastle in May, when having a rare run on Tapeta, and also on his Chester form since; headgear returns for the first time since last July.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced since getting off the mark on Wolverhampton AW (about 1m1f) last June, dropping out there 16 days ago for another new yard; has plenty to prove in his first 7f handicap.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Had 7f form in Ireland but that was two years ago and his course win came over 1m in April (made all) on second start for this yard; no advance on that form in two runs since but perhaps the return to this trip will improve his options.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Five AW wins include two on this course (at 6f; has won over 7f) but he's been going through a quieter spell of late; others more likely.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced; has shown enough on Polytrack to think he can find a race before long but he was well held in his first Tapeta handicap, over C&D recently, in first-time tongue-tie; will need more today.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden after 14 starts; very slowly away two starts ago but otherwise has possibilities, having run most of his best races on Tapeta and was hampered in his run over C&D last time; may well be involved.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr
Spotlight: Two 6f wins on Tapeta in 2025 and he's had just three goes at this trip, all on turf; he didn't do badly when fourth on Carlisle's stiff track last time out so perhaps he's worth another shot at 7f, albeit his AW mark is 5lb higher than on turf.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came over 1m here in 2024; on a useful mark at present but has a question mark about current form, having dropped out on his final start for previous yard and on debut for this one, at Chester 13 days ago.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Broke duck over 6f on Newcastle AW last November but he hasn't run to the same level at that trip or 7f this year; has it to prove at present.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runs; all three wins at 7f, the latest on Newcastle AW last August; probably found the return to 1m was pushing it on Kempton AW 15 days ago but he ran well for a long way and this test will work better for him.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Effective over C&D but his two wins this year have come over 1m on this track; has remained in form on turf since the latest of them in April and he shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Gleneagles colt cost 120,000euros as a yearling; second foal of a 1m winner (RPR 87), a half-sister to winners Helvezia (7f) and Caballero (7f 2yo); yard has a 16% strike rate with it's 2yos this season.

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Camelot half-brother to 6f 2yo winner The Flying Seagull (RPR 85); dam Listed-placed 5f winner (97), half-sister to winners Shevchenko (useful 7f/1m) and Tigris River (1m6f Flat and smart 2m-2m4f hurdle), out of an Irish 1,000 Guineas winner.

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Churchill colt; 40,000euros yearling; half-brother to winners Spirit Of Saxony (7f AW; RPR 89), Coolmeen Vega (Spanish 7.5f-8.5f) and Pure Circle (Italian 6f); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Jessica's Dream; stable second-choice on debut in Killarney maiden 10 days ago, showing definite promise when beaten just over 3l, tiring late over today's trip; could well improve enough to play a role (holds big-race entries).

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Dark Angel colt; 3rd foal of a 7f winner (RPR 81), a sister to useful 7f-2m winner Duty Of Care, half-sister to Breeders' Cup Mile winner Expert Eye; family of 1,000 Guineas winner Special Duty; yard having a good season with it's 2yos so newcomer of definite interest, especially if strong in the market (although no big-race entries).

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Dark Angel half-brother to US 6.5f/1m turf winner I'm Otter Here (inc 2yo); dam 1m winner (RPR 82), closely related to 1m Listed winner Zhui Feng, half-sister to winners Dominant (HK 1m4f Group 1) and Es Que Love (7f Group 2); yard had a debut 2yo winner at the Curragh last Saturday so has to be respected.

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Dubawi colt cost 1,300,000gns as a yearling, dam a 7f-1m2f winner (inc AW/Group 1; RPR 120) and half-sister to Speak In Colours (6f Group 2 winner); stable third-choice but plenty of debut promise in Curragh maiden in late June, staying on well after racing keenly early on to finish third behind two stablemates (7f); extra furlong should suit and could prove hard to beat.

RaceName: Frank Conroy Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Inns Of Court gelding is first foal of a 1m/9.5f winner (inc AW; RPR 61), a half-sister to winners Fearless Angel (Listed-placed 5f 2yo), Go Vince Go (5f/6f) and Dark Oak (1m4f); debutant best watched.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At Raceday-Ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: James Paul Services To Industry Ltd
Spotlight: Well beaten when sent off favourite on his debut in May but took a big step forward over this C&D last week (good to firm), making all in a first-time hood (retained); carries a penalty but he's the one to beat.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At Raceday-Ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50,000euros foal, £78,000 breeze-up buy; Blackbeard colt; second foal; dam 5f/6f winner (including AW; RPR 81), half-sister to winners A Momentofmadness (useful 5f/6f including 2yo) and Arc Royal (7f 2yo), out of 6f 2yo winner, is granddaughter of 6f 2yo Group 3 winner; has a speedy pedigree and he's one to be interested in on his debut.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At Raceday-Ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tongue-tied for two previous starts but not today; pulled too hard when fourth of 13 on his debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) and again took a firm grip before fading into eighth at Doncaster (6f, good to firm); this drop in trip could help him settle better.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At Raceday-Ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Filly by Harry Angel; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Simmering (6f/7f 2yo including Group 2; RPR 108) and Estate (5f; 84); dam 7f 2yo winner (82), closely related to 5f-7.4f winner Muatadel, out of 6f Group 3 winner; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Both wins of 20-race career came over fences back-to-back 18 months ago when landing competitive handicaps at Leopardstown over 2m1f; largely struggled since, pulled up on last two starts; last run over hurdles in October when beaten 16l in novice; up in trip and rated 10lb lower over hurdles; questions attached and yard perhaps holds better claims with Sean Mc.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some promise for Gordon Elliott when placed in two maiden hurdles last year; well-beaten on stable debut over C&D 57 days ago; switched back to handicaps now in first-time tongue-tie.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Some decent efforts placed twice in maidens and went close off 1lb higher when second at Galway (2m1f, y) in October; below form on soft since and fared no better on more suitable ground at Limerick 21 days ago after a break in first-time cheekpieces; needs to come on for that up in trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner, latest at Bellewstown (3m, yielding) in April off 5lb lower; pulled up and well-held since; needs more dropped in trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won three handicap hurdles on varying ground in 2023; good runs in 2024 but below form last year; improved on Punchestown return to be beaten 11.5l into fifth at Cork 13 days ago; holds each-way claims but needs more off this mark.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Breakthrough after some good placed runs over C&D in April, beating Cowper Hall by 2l on good; below form next time but shaped well when beaten 10.5l into third at Listowel 54 days ago; could be more to come in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 13-race maiden but has shaped well on a few occasions, most notably when fourth at Leopardstown (2m2f, soft) in March; filled same spot on last hurdles run at Cork on good in May; below that over fences last twice; chance back over hurdles with trip to suit in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Good second in Naas maiden hurdle in December; looked on a workable mark the last twice at Cork (3m, gd) and Ballinrobe (2m6f, gd) when third and fourth; drop in trip likely to suit and can figure.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't progressed from mildly promising sixth on debut in Leopardstown maiden hurdle; beaten a long way at Roscommon 17 days ago; bit to prove down in trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed a bit more when seventh in handicap (2m4f, hvy) at Fairyhouse but failed to progress upped to 3m2f next time; dropped another 3lb; bumper form on better ground gives hope conditions can bring improvement down in trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner whose best run came at Sligo last month when beaten 3.5l into third over extended 3m1f on yielding; couldn't back that up 11 days ago; tries a shorter trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won off this mark at Sligo (extended 2m4f, gd) June 2025; some good runs since but well beaten at Ballinrobe 58 days ago; needs to bounce back but type to do so.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Improved upped in trip for handicap debut when beaten 11.5l into fourth at Downpatrick (extended 2m5f, y) in March; step backwards in first-time cheekpieces 21 days ago; dropped 2lb and drops in trip.

RaceName: Wexford Racecourse Ladies Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sole win came at Tramore (2m5f, y) April 2025 off 1lb higher; struggled a bit since but finished to good effect over 2m3f on good at Clonmel in June when sixth; weakened into 10th at Limerick 21 days ago; drop in trip should suit.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up three times in a row on AW this April-June, but below-par sixth at Kempton (1m, AW) three weeks ago and he failed to shine when tackling turf last year; has his first outing on grass of 2026.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who hasn't progressed since making a promising start to her career last year; however, there have been hints that this first crack at 1m will suit, when keeping on for fifth at both Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January and Leicester (6f, good to firm) this month; not discounted.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who won at Kempton (1m, AW) last October; however, this 8yo wasn't at the top of his game at Southwell (1m, AW) in January and he may improve for this first outing since.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Having dropped down the weights he returned to form when third at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last month; however, a lesser run followed at Ascot (7f, good to firm) and he's unproven at 1m.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Won on reappearance at Bath (1m, firm) in April in first-time blinkers (retained); some respectable efforts since but needs something extra if he's to return to winning ways today.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
OwnerName: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner
Spotlight: Back on song when runner-up at Lingfield (7f, good) last month, but he's struggled on both runs since; very awkward at the start and soon tailed off when 40-1 over C&D (good) three weeks ago; risky.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Patchy campaign last season but he's 2lb lower than when winning at Newbury (1m, good) last May and reappeared with a good second at Brighton (1m, good to firm) last month; in the mix if backing that up.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Went close at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) on reappearance and returned to form to win at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) recently; he likes that seaside venue and drops back in trip today, but he is capable of running well away from Brighton and at 1m, so he could be in the shake-up.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Bath (1m, good) in May on third start for new yard and completed the hat-trick at Epsom (1m2f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; had just a neck to spare last time but he's just 3lb higher today and there can be optimism that this smooth-travelling 3yo still has more left in the tank.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Promise on both 2yo starts (7f, AW) and, following wind surgery, he kept on for a close second on last month's handicap debut at Ffos Las (7.3f, good) where he had to wait for a clear run; open to further improvement back up to 1m and this 3yo is on the shortlist.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form when runner-up at Kempton (1m, AW) in May, but down the field at Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) since and she's won just one of her 18 starts; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Billy Ocean Live At Sandown Park 29 July Handicap
Spotlight: He's dropped down the weights and didn't run badly when third of seven at Brighton (7f, good to firm) recently, but he's 0-12 in handicaps and needs a bigger performance if he's to come out on top today.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Front-running filly whose RPRs are moving in the right direction; dominated a small-field maiden at Ripon (1m, good to firm) 17 days ago and her pedigree points to this move up to 1m2f suiting now handicapping.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who showed his opening mark to be workable when 1l fourth over 1m2f at Windsor (good to firm) ten days ago, albeit it came in a steadily run race; first-time blinkers go on now.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Struggled on her first two outings this year but back on track stepped up to 1m2f when third in 11-runner handicap at Newbury (good to firm) three weeks ago, staying on having had to switch to get a run; the first two from that race have run well since; considered from an unchanged mark.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has failed to build on his 1m course handicap debut third in the spring in two subsequent outings, including stepped up to 1m2f at Ripon last time.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Similar form in three qualifying runs over 7f/1m last autumn; gelded ahead of this handicap debut and reappearance over a trip which is bred to suit; would enter calculations if the betting vibes are strong.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to better modest form in five outings as a juvenile, including a couple of handicaps; a watching brief is the percentage call after ten months off.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: 160,000gns yearling by Bated Breath; a weak 16-1 chance when finishing a remote sixth at Windsor (1m, good to soft); has to show more.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Finished last on debut here and again at Thirsk, both times over 1m; no appeal.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
OwnerName: Peter Onslow, FDC Holdings Ltd & NWIR Ltd
Spotlight: Ran okay when beaten 8.5l in a warm race at York (1m) but Newcastle (7f) was disappointing; not without hope if tapping back into the promise of that initial run.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: 155,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; 28-1, he showed promise behind a talented stablemate over 1m here a month ago (beaten a shade under 7l); can improve; tongue-tie goes on.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Beaten about 12l into midfield at Wolverhampton last month (7f) where he didn't get the clearest of passages; still makes limited appeal.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Night Of Thunder colt who cost 100,000gns as a yearling and 580,000euros at a breeze-up sale; half-brother to seven winners including Via Serendipity (7f-8.6f; RPR 111), Strong Chemistry (7f; 107) and Le Juge (1m-1m2f; 106); dam 7f/1m Listed winner (101); lots to like on paper and the market can guide as to expectations on debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: 4,000euros foal; fifth foal; half-brother to Bahraini 7f winner Yuan Prince; dam 7f winner (RPR 71), sister to 5f Group 1 winner Move In Time; gelded ahead of this debut and a hood is fitted; not that appealing.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Well beaten in two runs for James Owen and was sold on for 1,000gns.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Down the field in 1m/7f races at Newcastle, the latest in early May; no appeal.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Beaten three times but she sets the standard on her thirds at Carlisle (1m, soft) and Ffos Las (extended 7f, good); last time she was clear of the rest behind two promising horses.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has performed to an ordinary level thus far and handicaps will be more like it.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
Spotlight: From a good family, being a half-sister to useful middle-distance/hurdles winner Redicean and smart Haunted Dream, among others; may ultimately want more of a stamina test.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div I)
OwnerName: Gestuet Schlenderhan Gmbh U Co Kg
Spotlight: By Sea The Stars; first foal; dam German 1m3f winner (Group 3; RPR 106), half-sister to French 1m4f winner Waugh; bred to be above average and the market should be informative.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Pinatubo filly cost 300,000euros at the Arqana breeze-ups; half-sister to winners Golden Bugle (1m4f/13.5f; RPR 90) and Golden Love (1m; 59); dam 1m winner (inc Listed; 107), half-sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Footstepsinthesand and 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Pedro The Great; holds Lowther and Moyglare entries; obvious appeal from top yard.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Olympiad filly cost 65,000USD as a yearling, 300,000USD at Ocala in March; early speed before fading quite badly on Listowel debut in late May behind a stablemate; much more needed.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Pinatubo filly is second foal; dam placed 1m/9.4f (RPR 71), half-sister to winners Eternal Silence (Group-placed 1m) and Chiara Luna (useful 6f/8.5f inc 2yo), out of 1m4f Group 2 winning half-sister to 1m6f Group 1 winner Royal Diamond; market well worth a check on debut.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: State Of Rest filly cost 34,000euros as a foal; half-sister to winners Loaded Gun (5f-1m2f inc AW/2yo/Qatar; RPR 81) and Another Bertie (5f/6f AW; 54); dam 7f 2yo winner (85), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Roxan; rare 2yo runner for the yard and likely all the better for the experience.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Naval Crown filly has put together series of good runs in defeat, including nose C&D defeat last month, most recently front-running clear third over C&D a week ago; will again run her race but remains vulnerable to above-average newcomers.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showcasing filly is first foal of a 1m AW winner (RPR 91), who is closely related to Group-placed 1m1f 2yo winner Green Storm; yard have a 20% strike rate with it's 2yos this season.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Bayside Boy filly cost 80,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Wonderful Times (1m2f inc AW/French Listed; RPR 101), Wondrous Words (1m2f; 89) and Megalithic (Qatari 7f); dam 6f/7f (Group 3) 2yo winner (105), sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Gustav Klimt; quite nicely-bred newcomer, worth a market check.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well in a barrier trial but failed to run to market expectations over C&D last month, having reportedly got upset in the stalls; improvement likely.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Hello Youmzain filly cost 6,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to French 1m4f winner Imperious Man; dam French 8.5f AW winner, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Sofast; yard had a 2yo winner at Down Royal last week.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam placed 6f 2yo, half-sister to winners Oh Purple Reign (Listed-placed 6f-1m) and Warsaw Road (7f); dam placed 6f 2yo (70); shaped with some promise on Bellewstown debut early this month but this looks a stronger race.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Earthlight filly cost 20,000euros; couple of ordinary efforts in 5f maidens, now upped markedly in trip; more likely one for a nursery.

RaceName: Irish EBF Median Sires Series Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Soldier's Call filly; fourth foal; dam poor maiden half-sister to several winners, notably Fanaar (1m UAE Group 3); first 2yo runner of the season for the yard, seems best watched.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced since making a winning debut at Salisbury (7f, good) in 2024 but ran creditably when ridden by Ebrahim Nader at Newbury (1m, good to firm) two weeks ago; that was his first outing for nine months and he may well come on for it; claims.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Five of his six wins have been on the AW and the other came here over 7f last May (good to soft); well beaten on his last three starts, including at Newmarket last time on his first start for ten months, and is now tried in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: All six wins have been on the AW, the most recent coming at Southwell (1m, AW) in February; he was in fair form when last seen four months ago but has failed to beat a rival home in each of his three starts on turf.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Seven-time winner when trained by Rae Guest and posted respectable efforts on the AW over 1m2f in two of his three outings for Edward Smyth-Osbourne; has been off since January and drops back in trip on his first start for this yard.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came in a Lingfield maiden (1m, AW) in September on his final start for George Boughey; runner-up twice on the AW at the beginning of the year, and he may have needed his first start since February at Epsom (7f, good to firm) two weeks ago.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came at Chester (7f, good; first-time hood) on her stable debut last July and she's posted some good efforts since, including at Brighton (1m) on her last two starts (carried head awkwardly latest); solid contender.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Best efforts have been over C&D and he ran well when second of five here in a first-time tongue-tie (retained) three weeks ago; he's on a handy mark and can go well.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but placed six times in maidens and made no impact on initial handicap runs but fared better when beaten 8l into fourth at Limerick lto; claims of same mark with step up in trip likely to suit on evidence.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Ran to a similar level in three maidens and handicaps; poorly placed when a well-held fifth at this track 57 days ago; dropped 4lb, up in trip in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Went close at Perth and Galway over this trip last year but well below that form in two starts this year, pulled up at Cartmel 27 days ago; needs more in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Winner at Listowel (23.5f, gd) May last year and placed twice later in the year; three poor runs this term, however, and needs a bit of a revival.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner who is 0-6 over hurdles and ran poorly at Downpatrick nine days ago; switch to handicaps will suit but needs more.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner in November, who ran okay when slowly away at Sligo (2m2f, y) last month but pulled up on handicap debut 13 days ago; cheekpieces go on and needs to bounce back quickly up in trip.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Not disgraced in six starts in maidens and was running well before falling two out at Gowran Park in March; wears hood on handicap debut up in trip.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Point winner who improved to be beaten 1.5l into second over C&D in May by Lecale's Diamond (good to yielding) and form hasn't worked out bad; couldn't repeat that effort off 3lb higher when well-backed favourite at Downpatrick next time but race wasn't run to suit and he's definitely worth another chance; leading player back up in trip.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: No impact in six maiden hurdles and ran poorly on handicap debut 19 days ago; hard to make a case for.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Steadily improving and good run when 1.5l second to Love Like This at Kilbeggan (3m, gd) last month; failed to back that up at Roscommon last time but strong claims if bouncing back in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Little to no impact in four maiden hurdles; steps up in trip in first-time cheekpieces on handicap debut; market can guide.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Breakthrough win at Clonmel (2m4f, good to yielding) in May; form of that has worked out with her pulling clear with Global Skies who won his next two; well-held second on soft next time and ran just okay when fifth at Limerick 21 days ago; capable of a big run but needs to reverse form with Set The Tone.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Was struggling in handicaps prior to a big step in the right direction when beaten 6.5l by subsequent winner Knocknagoran Lady at Limerick (2m7f, gd) 21 days ago with Hob's Angel behind in fifth; one to beat if building on that.

RaceName: Follow BetVictor On Instagram Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Delivered on earlier promise and looked suited by the step up in trip when making all in Lingfield novice (7f, AW) this month; returns to turf for this nursery debut; likely contender again.

RaceName: Follow BetVictor On Instagram Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Scored on debut at Salisbury (6f, soft) in June and also ran well when third in quite valuable conditions race over that C&D (good to firm) three weeks later; upped in trip for nursery debut, having recently been gelded; ought to make his presence felt.

RaceName: Follow BetVictor On Instagram Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Travelled strongly before asserting in pretty good style at Beverley (7.4f, good; third a recent winner) last month, his second start; the only runner from Karl Burke's North Yorkshire stable on this card; needs to be taken very seriously.

RaceName: Follow BetVictor On Instagram Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Swooped on the outside for comfortable success in Chester novice (6f, good to soft) last month, his second start, and seemed to cope with the much faster ground when respectable fourth at Salisbury (6f, good to firm; 1.5l behind Flying Pirate but now 3lb better off) on latest outing; probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Follow BetVictor On Instagram Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Built upon promising debut when finishing well to collar a subsequent winner at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; did that quite cosily in the end and could easily have more improvement to come in nurseries back on turf.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Half-brother to 6f/7f winner Deposer (RPR 112); this is a very belated debut at six and can only be watched.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Insufficient promise in her first two races to warrant a second look in this.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Australian-bred filly by Frankel; had one run in these colours for George Boughey when beaten a little under 10l in a warm 1m novice for fillies at Newmarket just over a year ago; second run has been a long time coming but she has ability.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Well related; showed plenty more on his second visit to Kempton (7f) when only 2l behind a Ralph Beckett-trained winner; this colt raced prominently before weakening; solid chance on the back of that.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Trailed the winner by about 20l on 7f debut at Wolverhampton two weeks ago (250-1).

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
OwnerName: A D G Oldrey & G C Hartigan
Spotlight: Gelded after a very quiet debut last November and duly showed lots more when just run out of it at Lingfield (extended 7f, soft) having been given every chance; that wasn't a strong race despite the winner following up in a handicap, but neither is this.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Half-brother to 5.7f winner Malham Tarn Cove (RPR 57); dam 5f/6f winner (68); kicks off at 7f despite his speedy pedigree; limited appeal.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: High-class US pedigree and cost 250,000euros at a 2yo breeze-up sale; however, changed hands again for 6,000gns this April; a watching brief is best.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Last of six on debut and a tailed-off last of nine last week at Wolverhampton (first-time hood).

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Half-brother to five winners including Free Love (5f; RPR 92), Lydia's Place (5f; 90), Lawless Louis (5f-6f; 81) and Dimitar (7f/1m; 76); dam 5f 2yo winner (74); gelded ahead of debut; one to watch more than likely.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: They all finished in a bit of a heap when this filly was only 2.5l off the winner here 18 days ago; ran well for a 250-1 chance but probably needs more here.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to five winners including Earls Rock (US 6.5f-8.5f; Grade 3; RPR 107), Lady La Fay (11.4f/1m4f; 94) and Cash Or Crypto (6f; 88); dam 7.4f/1m winner (97); trainer-owned newcomer; others preferred.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App 'Confined' Maiden Stakes (No More Than Three Runs) (GBB) (Div II)
OwnerName: J W Parry And Mrs C Shekells
Spotlight: Respectable runs at Chelmsford and here but hard to rate her form too highly and she's stepping up from 5f.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
OwnerName: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
Spotlight: Sea The Stars filly out of the brilliant Alpha Centauri; debut second over C&D last month to Blonde Over Blue (soft) before bolting up over the same trip on quicker ground in Curragh maiden later in month, making all (second beaten odds-on favourite since, third has won since) to win under hands and heels; high-class filly in the making, major player.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 260,000gns as a foal and 400,000gns as a yearling; first foal; dam 1m1f-1m4f winner (inc AW/Italian Group 3; RPR 105); not especially strong in the market when making winning debut last month in C&D fillies' maiden, handy throughout off steady pace and getting first run on strong-finishing second Alpha; very interesting rematch with that rival who  has won impressively since.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: By Prix de l'Abbaye winner Wooded and cost 41,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to French 6f-7.5f winner Viva La Skids (inc 2yo; RPR 103); dam maiden, is out of half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Desert Blanc; has missed two engagements (coughing/off feed); eye-catching jockey booking for belated debut.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Magna Grecia filly won a Roscommon maiden and most recently nicely improved to land Naas Listed over a mile, relishing stamina test; going the right way but this likely a much stiffer task.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: No Nay Never half-sister to Group 1 juvenile winner Puerto Rico; steady improvement in three maiden runs, most recently third over C&D a fortnight ago; that form looks a long way off what is likely required here and cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: Saudi Cup Silver Flash Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly took a step in the right direction in Naas Listed early this month when staying on fifth (beaten 3.5l) behind Dancing Destiny over 1m; lots more required here.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Frank Barry & Mrs Luke Barry
Spotlight: Made a winning stable debut in a Southwell novice (6f, AW) in January and took another step forward when sixth of eight in a Listed event at Lingfield (7f, AW) next time; now makes her handicap debut having been off since failing to beat a rival in a Goodwood Listed event in May and still needs to prove herself on turf.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: All six starts have been on the AW; won at Lingfield (7f) in April before a good second of 13 at Kempton next time, and ran respectably in a 0-105 at Newcastle (7f) last month; drops in grade and has strong claims if coping with the surface on his turf debut.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins from four starts for Karl Burke (6f/7f; AW); ran well when third at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) on last month's stable and turf debut, but she was well held at Newmarket two weeks ago (hung left); needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Won 6f maiden and novice events as a 2yo; has posted three good efforts since being gelded, the most recent when narrowly denied in a three-runner handicap at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) four weeks ago; contender.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in 2024 but has been out of form a long time now and switch to this stable in his last two runs hasn't helped; sole hope is that this is his first time dropping into this grade since his win.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Point winner, ordinary form in maiden hurdles, although one of her better efforts came over 2m4f here in May when a 7l fourth; pulled up on handicap debut at Limerick last time (2m4f, good); cheekpieces go on now and local trainer's record around here speaks for itself.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Her sole win and all three places have come at Downpatrick; another good third there in June but efforts elsewhere are concerning, in rear when unseated at three out at Kilbeggan last time.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Fourth in this last year before winning handicaps at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) and Down Royal (3m, good to firm); pulled up on seasonal return at Clonmel in June (2m7f, good) but much more promising was her 8l third of 18 at Limerick last time (2m7f, good), despite making a bad mistake; interesting.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a Sligo maiden hurdle last summer but hasn't been near that level in handicaps since, though her C&D run here in May was one of her better efforts; cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Hard to fancy on her form for Patrick Foley; makes handicap/stable debut now.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Went close at Down Royal (3m1f, good to yielding) off 2lb lower in Oct' but failed to build on that in two runs on soft afterwards; made a promising seasonal return at Kilbeggan (3m1f, good to yielding) when a 7.75l fifth of 14; pulled up at Roscommon last time; blinkers go on now.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Beaten 21l on handicap debut at Downpatrick earlier this month (2m2f, good), staying on late; this step up in trip looks like the right move; not ruled out.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles with her best finish being second at Punchestown (3m, good) in May off this mark; below that level in two runs since; cheekpieces go on.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: No signs of winning potential yet in five bumpers and four runs over hurdles; beaten 25l in a C&D maiden hurdle last time; goes handicapping now but others preferred.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Pulled up in previous four outings before pulling off a shock success at Bellewstown last time (2m5f, good, 50-1), running on well; record suggests he can't be trusted to follow up off 6lb higher, but can't be dismissed all the same.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Winless from 14 starts over hurdles but her best placing came over C&D last summer when second off 7lb higher; some promise in her 9l fourth at Sligo on penultimate; on the retreat when a faller at two out at Limerick last time but a positive effort all the same; chance.

RaceName: Cottage Autism Network Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Has lost way since a purple patch in 2024; didn't build on okay seasonal return at Clonmel when tailed off at Tramore last time.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Out of a Group-placed 6f 2yo winner and made quite promising debut when sixth of 13 here (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; likely to play a significant role if able to build on that performance.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 86,000euros yearling; first foal; dam runner-up 7f/1m AW (RPR 68), half-sister to winners Emperor Claudius (1m Listed), Dark Lochnagar (1m1f 2yo/1m2f) and Dove Mountain (7f-1m2f), out of 1,000 Guineas winner Virginia Waters; market may guide.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Out of a very well-connected mare but was towards the rear throughout her debut at Kempton (7f, AW) six weeks ago and needs to take a big step forward here.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not disgraced when seventh in good Newmarket maiden (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago, her second run, but needs to improve again here.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; sister to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Publish (RPR 96); dam US 1m-1m1f turf winner (including Grade 2; 106), sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winners Arizona; with a top stable and looks the part on paper.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 5,000gns foal; first foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to winners Divine Unicorn (Australian 1m3f Group 3) and Measuring Time (useful 7f 2yo), out of disqualified 1m 2yo winner/Oaks fourth; the other three newcomers look more interesting.

RaceName: Pump Technology Services Jung Training Day EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to 6f/7f 2yo winner Ellaria Sand (incuding Listed; RPR 97), half-sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Ba'Hoa (76); dam 6f AW winner (79), half-sister to useful winners Rekdhat (7f/1m including 2yo) and Ajaadat (7.4f/1m including 2yo); has the right type of pedigree for this race.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: February foal by Starspangledbanner; 20,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners La Ferveur (French 8.5f; RPR 88) and Impelling (Czech 6.5f); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (94), half-sister to Italian 5f Listed winner Gamgoom, out of half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Profitable; already gelded and would be a very rare winning 2yo newcomer from the yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Improved plenty from his debut effort when beaten just over a length into third of 18 at Newbury (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; half-brother and dam both won on the AW and any further improvement will make him a big player.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 25-1 when seventh of ten on his Ayr debut (7f, good) this month; improvement needed, though he is a half-brother to two AW winners and his sire has a good record around here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: K Shenton, N Bradley 6 & D Cunha
Spotlight: Some ability in his first two starts, but failed to beat a rival at Bath (5.7f, firm) this month (reportedly hung violently right-handed); gelding operation will need to have made a big difference.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: April foal by Calyx; 25,000euros yearling; half-brother to six winners including Whispering Bell (1m; RPR 90), Stardayz (1m AW; 81), Unsung Hero (7f AW; 78) and Beauty Nation (6f AW 2yo; 78); dam placed 5f 2yo (72), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Newfangled; sire has a good record here and stable does have winning 2yo newcomers; worth a market check.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: All four starts in Class 2 company, including when beaten a neck by the subsequent Lily Agnes winner Adonius at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) in April and when third of 13 in the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) last month; high draw one small negative, but he is in much calmer waters here and his four winning siblings and his dam were all successful on the AW; should be hard to beat.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Nice pedigree but well held in 6f maidens at Windsor (good to firm) and Kempton (Polytrack) last month; nurseries beckon.
