RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Faced an impossible task on last month's York debut as things turned out, but did hint at ability when fifth of ten at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) 12 days later; not sure dropping back in trip is ideal, though, and nurseries over further may see him in a better light.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Completely blew the start when last of nine on his debut in a Leicester seller (6f, good to firm; 11-1) this month; needs a sizeable step forward.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed some promise when fourth of eight on last month's Carlisle debut (5f, good to soft), but the form hasn't worked out and he himself was well held at Pontefract (6f, good) 12 days later; has the outside stall and may be one for nurseries.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 25-1 when last of nine on his Newcastle debut (7f, Tapeta) four weeks ago; look elsewhere.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Held in her first two starts at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) and Ripon (6f, good), but showed a glimmer of ability on each occasion; out of a Listed winner, but nurseries may be a more suitable option after this.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Superbly bred colt was the second-string on debut when winning a Curragh maiden over this trip; his better fancied stablemate, Giant Sequoia, finished third and has since won a Curragh maiden; reared in the stalls and was withdrawn before the start of the Chesham Stakes when 11/4; a big player if there is no repeat of that behaviour; stable first-string.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Finished third over C&D on debut and improved from that to take a Fairyhouse maiden over 7f; seemed to get outstayed over 1m in a Naas listed race last time and coming back to this trip can suit; looks the stable second-string but can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Shaped with promise on debut at the Curragh over 6f and was much-improved to run away with a Gowran maiden over 7f since; the form of that race is solid and if this colt is as effective on this sounder surface then he should be a major player; looks the yard first-string.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: An 80,000gns yearling; started 3/1 second fav' for a 6f Fairyhouse maiden and showed a good attitude to score, beating Supreme Lord into third by 1l; the fourth that day won at the Curragh last weekend and this colt should have improved since; seems stable second-string.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Cost 400,000gns as a yearling; started 5/2 fav' and ran a solid race on debut at Fairyhouse over 6f, beaten 1l by Nail House; and this colt should have improved since so there shouldn't be much between them today.

RaceName: Japan Racing Association Tyros Stakes (Group 3)
Spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 6f turf/7f AW winner Ze Do Asfalto (RPR 84); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (78), sister to 5f/6f winner Awsaaf, half-sister to prolific 5f/6f winner Street Life; second runner for yard; decent pedigree but will have to be a smart sort to take this on debut.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Gained his sixth success when winning at Kempton (7f, AW) in March on his reappearance but he's been below that level in both starts since, including at Newbury three weeks ago; drops in grade but others have stronger claims.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice at around 1m in June 2024; never dangerous when eighth of 12 in a 0-100 at York (6f, good) last month and she possibly wasn't helped by racing in a small group of three at Newbury (7f, good to firm) three weeks ago; claims in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Three of his four wins have been on the AW and the other came over this C&D in May off a 3lb lower mark than today's; ran respectably over 1m here four weeks ago and holds solid claims back down in trip.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Without a win since his debut in 2024 (posted an RPR of 89) but he was placed in each of his three starts for Jack Jones, and went close over C&D on his stable debut four weeks ago; he's on a handy mark and has to be respected.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Won on the AW over 7f at Chelmsford in January on his stable debut but he's been below his best on turf in recent starts, including when last of six over 1m here last month; visor now applied.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Came home nicely to win at Chelmsford (7f, AW; first start for 95 days) in January and was returning from a 50-day break when winning over 1m here (good to firm) three weeks ago; goes up in grade off a 3lb higher mark and will need them to go a solid gallop over this shorter trip.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner with the latest coming over C&D in March last year (good to yielding) off 4lb higher; not too much encouragement to be taken from his two most recent hurdles runs on soft conditions but could do better back on summer ground.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Downpatrick handicap hurdle winner off 89 in 2024 (2m2f, good); signed off his time with Paul Flynn with a poor effort at Musselburgh (2m, good to soft) when last seen in February last year; mammoth absence to overcome in a first-time tongue tie.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden's best run was when third in a Gowran handicap in October (2m4f, yielding); tailed off at Fairyhouse in December and pulled up on return at Limerick in April (2m4f, soft to heavy)

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Won over C&D in May last year and second in a similar event next time off 99; decent C&D third last time (good) and should be in the mix again.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Back-to-back winner at Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding) and Down Royal (2m2f, good) in April/May; decent C&D effort on penultimate when a clear runner-up off this mark; pulled up here last time though, so hard to know what to expect now.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Sixth of 18 over hurdles at Punchestown in May (2m, good to yielding) was her best hurdles run, but she didn't build on that at Limerick last time when tailed off (2m4f, soft); probably best on a sound surface.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Didn't improve for switch to handicaps over C&D last time when 22l behind, reluctant to race at start; others preferred.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Didn't land a blow on handicap debut at Listowel; better over C&D last time when a 4.5l sixth of 13, fading late on; not completely ruled out.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: First-time blinkers worked the oracle over C&D last time when he left previous poor form behind to win by 3.25l (good); up 6lb but big chance if the headgear retains the same magic.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles and not much encouragement to take from his recent outings.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Flat winner has run creditably in defeat recently; missed the start at Sligo (2m2f, good, 7-2 fav) but did well to keep on and take third; went one place better at Clonmel last time when 2.25l behind the winner; a win is coming if her consistency continues and O'Keeffe rides for the first time.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden from 22 starts (18 over hurdles); beaten 17.5l on his return at Downpatrick in May; makes stable debut now but others preferred.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner is 0-15 over hurdles but he took second over C&D (good, 20-1) earlier this month; chance if he builds on that but his overall form suggests he might not.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Made good progress last year, collecting four 6f wins along the way, but made nothing better than a satisfactory reappearance in May and is still on a fairly tough mark for this first run since.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Made all off today's mark on seasonal debut at Bath (5.7f, firm) in April and will be fine over this slightly longer trip but doesn't always looks straightforward and needs to up her game after two below-par 5f runs.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Bettered disappointing seasonal debut when third of five at Nottingham (6f, good) last month and is now 1lb lower than when winning from off the pace at Haydock (6f, good) last summer; no surprise if she features.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Still 4lb higher than when winning at Leicester (6f, good to firm) in May but has been pretty consistent since rejoining Tony Carroll in March and probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Sightly disappointing after sluggish start at Newmarket this month but returned to turf with three good runs this spring, the most notable a win at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on second occasion; features prominently in calculations.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive 3yo who opened her account in five-runner Yarmouth handicap (6f, good to firm) last month, her fifth start; 5lb higher in a deeper race here but should still have more to offer; high on the list.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Scored on seasonall/handicap debut at Southwell (6f, AW) in April and made the frame three consecutive times on turf (5f-6f) afterwards; didn't fire last time but remains quite lightly raced; contender if back on song.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: 4-13 on AW but 0-11 on turf; had soft ground as an excuse last time, though, and has each-way claims at least if judged on her second at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May.

RaceName: Country & Town House Handicap
Spotlight: Quirky 4yo with who often starts slowly and/or races too freely, and refused to race three outings ago; did well to win at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last month and will be suited by this return to 6f but others are much less risky.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Well held in novices at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last month; one of his winning half-sisters and his dam were successful on the AW, but best watched for now.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 50-1 when beaten 2l into fourth of nine on this month's Leicester debut (6f, good to firm); that was only a seller, though, and others are preferred even though the third has won since.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 40-1 when well beaten on this month's Thirsk debut (6f, good to firm); stable's newcomers are usually better for a run, but he needs plenty of improvement.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Hasn't stepped up from his narrow defeat at Pontefract (5f, good) on his second start in April, albeit 7f may have stretched him at Ayr last time; has still shown enough to make the shortlist, though, with his half-brother and dam both successful on the AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 16-1 when beating only one home on his Leicester debut (5f, good to firm) in May; hard to recommend.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Missed the break but still showed plenty of promise when third of ten on her Yarmouth debut (6f, good to firm; 33-1) five weeks ago (fifth has won since); wouldn't want to lose too much ground from the inside stall here, but still a major player granted normal progress.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 25-1 when finishing in midfield behind the subsequent Super Sprint winner Zigazig Ah on last month's Yarmouth debut (6f, good to firm); needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: April foal by Dream Ahead; half-sister to winners Miss Mai Tai (6f-1m including 2yo/France/Czechia; RPR 89), Miss Woo Woo (6f-7.5f including 2yo/AW/France/Belgium; 76) and Sands Of Seve (5f AW; 57); dam once-raced (52) half-sister to Swedish 5f-1m winner Sweet Loreen; would be a very rare winning 2yo newcomer from the yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Had excuses for her Wetherby debut, but less so for her Newbury effort (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; best watched for now.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Surrounded by subsequent winners when third of 11 on her Beverley debut (5f, good to soft) in May, but disappointed at Redcar (6f, good to firm) later in month; her dam won four times on the Fibresand here and she would have to be respected if building on that first effort.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: March foal by Belardo; 2,000gns yearling; dam unraced half-sister to winners Day Of Conquest (useful 7f/1m 2yo), Day By Day (useful 5f/6f) and Alexander Ballet (5f; dam of 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Hearts of Fire); stable has few 2yos go in first time while the outside stall may prove tricky for a newcomer.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C/D) (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Some ability when fourth of seven on her Musselburgh debut (5f, good) in May, but was completely out of her depth in the Woodcote at Epsom (6f, good to soft) last month; two of her siblings and her dam won on the AW, but nurseries may be a better fit after this.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time winner up to 1m2.5f; last win came at the Curragh in 2025 off 2lb higher; has been a bit hit and miss since but has faced some stiff tasks too; beaten 6.5l in the Ulster Oaks last time off this mark; needs more.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark on 10th start when winning a Dundalk maiden over 10.5f last time; raised 3lb for that but has run well in defeat at this venue before so is one to consider.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner for Ger Lyons ran his best race for this yard when fifth over 1m at this track last time; was staying on that day so this extra furlong should be a help; considered.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have come at this track over 1m in varying ground conditions; cracking effort back here last time over that trip; up 3lb but the extra furlong is a question.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Won an AW maiden over this trip last winter; cracking efforts first two starts this term on turf, over 1m at Naas and 1m2f at Navan; not as good at the Curragh last time off this mark.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark when winning a Killarney maiden over 1m recently; has run well over C&D before so should be fine on that score; the tongue-tie worn last time is retained; a player.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
Spotlight: Opened his account when emphatically winning a Down Royal handicap over 1m2.5f in May; got a 15lb hike for that and has found life difficult twice since; down 2lb.

RaceName: Dublin To Bahrain Race Series Handicap
OwnerName: Martin W Walsh & Tony Carey & Brian Murray & Mark Hopkins
Spotlight: Won a Dundalk maiden over 1m2.5f last November; best run since was when third in a 1m2f handicap here; never involved over 1m4f at the Curragh since; might find this trip a bit sharp.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed filly who ran to a similar level in three starts last year, but she was tailed off in a three-runner event at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) on her handicap debut four weeks ago; steps up in trip and she may yet do better.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
OwnerName: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar
Spotlight: Won twice last August, at Brighton (1m2f, good) and Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm); below her best on her next three outings and she was brought down at Brighton last month; unproven over this trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Well held in three novice/maiden events in April/May and he was beaten 20l on his handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; steps up to 1m4f with headgear discarded.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning at Thirsk (1m4f, good) in May off 5lb lower, then seemed stretched by 1m6f on soft at Nottingham; ran well when close second of three at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) last night and she will have to be respected if running today.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick when winning at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last June but he mostly hasn't been at his best since; respectable effort at Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) last time but he still needs to prove his stamina for this far.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: The addition of a tongue tie transformed her at Roscommon (2m, good) last time when running out an easy winner (7l back to Syosset); up 9lb but it was an impressive performance and she likely has more to offer.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Best hurdling effort came over C&D earlier this month when a 4.5l fifth (2m1f, good), keeping on late in the game; could have a part to play and Kennedy is back aboard.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles and his two runs in handicaps when last seen in this sphere in April/May were not encouraging; some positives in his Flat form this year, but his 27l defeat at Tramore last time was not one of them.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Showed moderate form for Gavin Cromwell but the switch of stable appeared to have a big effect on her at Cork last time when second of 23 (2m2f, good, 50-1), making up significant ground from the rear; contender with a repeat.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Flat winner ran her best race over hurdles when a distant third of 14 on handicap hurdle debut at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) last month; below that level at Roscommon (2m, good) last time when 21l behind Del Boys Diva.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Maiden and most of her best form has come at Tramore, second over hurdles there in August (2m1f, good) and third over fences in the autumn; back over hurdles after a 22l defeat in a handicap chase here last time; seventh 3.50 here on Wednesday.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Very modest form so far both over hurdles and on the Flat; beaten 16.5l at Tramore last time on Flat; others more likely.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Has found his form on Flat at Dundalk, winning there in Nov' and running decent races twice afterwards; took that good form and applied it to hurdles when third at Roscommon last time, 7l behind Del Boys Diva; 8lb better off with that rival now; chance.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Fair sixth on handicap debut (2m, good to yielding); 2m4f on soft ground didn't suit her at Fairyhouse when last seen in December; the tongue tie returns and the blinkers are new; declared reserve for 7.09 at Ballinrobe on Tuesday.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Only 2.5l away at Tramore (2m, good) but fared less well at Clonmel and Cork since; now 0-8 over hurdles and a visor is reached for.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle at Thurles in March 2025 but undistinguished form outside that; pulled up on her final start for Aengus King over C&D last time; new surroundings need to have had a big effect on her.

RaceName: Park Run At Wexford Saturday Mornings Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Things picked up when second at Tramore (2m, good) and even though she ran poorly at Sligo next time, her recent outing at Cork also held promise when fifth of 23 (2m2f, good); may get involved.

RaceName: Newbury Racecourse Resident DJ, Moonraker Disco Handicap
Spotlight: Nothing like the force of old but won comfortably on seasonal debut at Ffos Las (2m, good; first win since 2021) last month, despite refusing to settle in early stages; only second when odds-on to follow up in four-runner race at Doncaster (1m6f, good to firm; again raced too freely) soon afterwards but at least we know he's in good nick.

RaceName: Newbury Racecourse Resident DJ, Moonraker Disco Handicap
Spotlight: Regressive 8yo who has not been firing on all cylinders this season and was beaten over 14l when fourth of five at Lingfield (2m, good) last month; continues to slide down the weights but needs a revival.

RaceName: Newbury Racecourse Resident DJ, Moonraker Disco Handicap
OwnerName: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner
Spotlight: Prolific AW winner, most recently when darting 14l clear at Wolverhampton (2m) last month; narrowly exploited lower turf mark at Lingfield (2m, good to firm) last night and, even under a penalty, remains well treated on his AW exploits; will enter calculations if he runs.

RaceName: Newbury Racecourse Resident DJ, Moonraker Disco Handicap
Spotlight: Clear second when upped to 11.6f for seasonal/handicap debut at Bath this month and won readily over 1m6f there (again on firm) last week; carries 6lb penalty (due to go up by that amount anyway) here but probably still has more improvement to come; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Only one win (June 2024) in the last four seasons; there have been some close calls this year, including at this track in January and at Brighton on penultimate start, so he has each-way claims.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Gained all three of his wins over hurdles at Ludlow (in May 2022, 2023 and 2025); twice second there before falling on latest start, seven weeks ago; lightly raced on Flat, without registering any solid form but they were all with either very different trip and/or surface to today's.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
OwnerName: W Enticknap, B Ralph And Partner
Spotlight: Edging down the weights and this six-race maiden has taken minor honours in his three handicaps (two 1m6f and one at 1m2f); first of those was on Wolverhampton Tapeta; considered.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 12-race maiden; third off this career-low mark over about 1m4f at Windsor and Brighton in her two starts for new yard, which puts her in each-way calculations even though she tailed off (2m in December) in her only start on Tapeta.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in first-time blinkers; 0-11 on the Flat, which sees him continue down the weights but he's been soundly beaten in Polytrack handicaps (unraced on Tapeta) and Flat record over the last year has been uninspiring; visored first time (has tried cheekpieces as well as the blinkers).

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Often a front-runner and gained the last six of his seven wins on this Southwell AW, with the latest win in March; Elle-May Croot knows him especially well, he's back to his last winning mark and return to Southwell (latest run here was five starts ago) might spark something.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 1m4f AW classified at Wolverhampton in March and so nearly won another at Lingfield 15 days later; pulled up over hurdles in May on his only start since but a return to form can't be ruled out, so neither can he.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
OwnerName: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford
Spotlight: Often front-runner; sole win from 13 races was at Beverley (1m2f) in June 2025 off 4lb higher; out of sorts this season and although her latest start was a step back in the right direction, this first run beyond 1m2f poses an additional question.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win (13 races) came over 1m4f in a classified on Wolverhampton Tapeta in March; third in classifieds on three of her four starts since, with recent form not looking quite good enough to win this handicap but she raced wide last time and peak efforts give her a shout.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Ridden by Finlay Bassett on last eight outings; sole win from 17 starts came on her handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, AW) in December; runner-up on three of her next four starts, including here, but not so good in recent races and needs to perk up again.

RaceName: Dubai Racing Carnival Rated Race
Spotlight: Some promise in both 2yo runs; improved from his seasonal debut here to win a Curragh maiden, both over 1m2f; did well on latter occasion as had to wait for room; takes a major step up in trip today but has prospects of staying.

RaceName: Dubai Racing Carnival Rated Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to a German 1000 Guineas winner from a speedy damline; made a winning debut over 1m at Dundalk in January and the runner-up is now rated 78; tongue-tie added now up markedly in trip after a break; still has to be respected.

RaceName: Dubai Racing Carnival Rated Race
OwnerName: Mrs Richard Henry & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Won Dundalk handicaps over 1m and 1m2.5f in the spring; fine efforts in defeat in 1m4f handicaps here and at the Curragh since  behind Darius Dark; up another 2lb but has prospects of staying and a player.

RaceName: Dubai Racing Carnival Rated Race
Spotlight: Showed improved form to win a Limerick handicap over 1m4f last time in first-time cheekpieces (retained); raised 12lb for that but shapes as if this trip is within reach so has to enter calculations.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark when winning in first-time blinkers over C&D last month (soft) and shaped as if still in good form when fourth of 12 at Windsor (5f, good to firm) one month ago, leaving the impression he'll be well suited to stepping back up in trip; contender.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
OwnerName: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen
Spotlight: Five wins in 2025 include one over C&D; has shaped as if back in form on his last two starts, including when a front-running second of five at Leicester (6f, good to firm) 19 days ago; should go well.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) last May off a 5lb higher mark than today's; below her best when last seen 287 days ago and makes her stable debut having left Julia & Shelley Birkett (bought for 5,000gns).

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: 11-race maiden but she ran her best race when second of eight at Newbury (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago on her second start for this stable; steps back up in trip with claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Dual 6f AW winner for Martin Dunne, latterly at Wolverhampton last July; mostly struggled since and now makes his stable debut/reappearance having been secured for 14,000gns.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: 12-race maiden; went close in a handicap at Nottingham (5f, soft) last month but he raced freely in a classified event over this trip at Lingfield (good) a week later; has the hood back on and needs to settle better.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: 11-race maiden but she has been placed on three occasions, including when second of four at Lingfield (6f, good to firm) last month; has cheekpieces back on and makes the shortlist.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort in three novice/maiden events when fifth of ten at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month; didn't look suited by the drop to 5f on her handicap debut back there last time (tongue-tied for the first time) and now steps back up in trip.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Order of St George gelding; 20,000euros foal; tenth foal; half-brother to French 2m1f chase winner My Vega; dam placed French 2m1f hurdles, half-sister to top-class 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Vautour, out of prolific 7f-1m6f Flat and 1m7f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner; makes belated debut in a hood but has an interesting pedigree; check market.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Feel Like Dancing gelding; dam 5f winner (RPR 59), half-sister to useful 7f-1m6f winner Profit's Reality, out of 7f winner; not an obvious candidate on breeding.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Pulled up in a Cork maiden hurdle last November, but after a break, was much better in a Tramore bumper despite pulling hard; has a chance and top amateur rider booked but must settle better.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to six winners inc Prakasa (German 7f/1m inc 2yo/Group 3; RPR 101), Rippling Waters (1m AW; 82) and Provokator (French 2m1f-2m3f hurdle/chase); dam German 1m2f/1m3f winner; best watched on debut.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to French winners Jena Sport (15.5f) and Irish Sport (2m3f hurdle); dam French 1m2f-1m4f winner (RPR 86); trainer 1-13 in bumpers last five seasons.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Newcomer by the Motivator stallion For Fun, a Grade 1 winner over hurdles in France; cost 24,00euros at two; dam unraced half-sister to French/German 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase winner; family of smart miler Enrique; check market; declared reserve in 4.36 at Ballinrobe on Tuesday.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam failed to complete in four points, out of German 1m winner; fell at the fourth in her sole point-to-point and that came over a year and a half ago.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Third foal; dam maiden Flat and jumps, half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Sughera, out of maiden close relative of Dewhurst dead-heater Prince Of Dance; top lady rider aboard but not super appealing on paper.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Hexham (2m, soft, 4-1); others preferred.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten favourite and finished second in both starts to date; no excuses when 4.75l behind the winner at Tramore and ran to a similar level when beaten 1.25l over C&D last time; probably worth taking on.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Tailed off at Roscommon on debut; unlike to feature.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Pulled up in sole point but made an eye-catching rules debut at Kelso when travelling strongly and only losing second in the dying strides; didn't build on that when keen at Bellewstown last time.

RaceName: Save The Date Ladies Day At Wexford Races Friday 7th August INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Beaten 24l on bumper debut at Punchestown (2m, soft to heavy); refused to race here last time; risky proposition after that but she was just 9-2 that day.

RaceName: Rayner Bosch Car Service Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed filly who made winning debut in AW in Ireland in January (10.6f) and posted best of three runs for new stable when third of seven on last month's handicap debut at Goodwood (1m4f, good), form recently well advertised by the winner.

RaceName: Rayner Bosch Car Service Handicap
Spotlight: Not seen on the Flat since signing off for Ed Walker with narrow success in first-time blinkers at Salisbury (1m2f, good) in October but won a recent handicap hurdle for new connections (no headgear) and is unexposed over this sort of trip in this sphere.

RaceName: Rayner Bosch Car Service Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive 4yo who scored on seasonal debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) and has gone close on last two appearances; drew nicely clear of the others with next-time-out winner at Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) last month and is only 2lb higher here (due to go up another 2lb soon) but needs to prove himself on the faster ground.

RaceName: Rayner Bosch Car Service Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo who started off for Richard Hughes with emphatic 1m4f turf wins at Brighton (good to firm) and Epsom (good) this summer; lost out narrowly when odds-on at Kempton (1m3f, AW) two weeks ago, having been forced to make his own running in a tactical race, but remains of strong interest.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Looked likely to benefit from a step up in trip when running best race for previous yard over 1m2f on Newcastle AW in February; however, was well held on yard debut at Kempton (1m4f, AW) and he just plugged on at one pace at Windsor (about 1m3f) in May.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Revealed little in his qualifying runs over much shorter trips but he lost out only near the line on handicap debut over 1m4f at Ripon before overpowering his rivals in a classified event at Pontefract (1m4f, good to firm) last week; goes unpenalised and is due a 5lb rise in future; now having first run on sand but dam has bred two AW winners; good claims.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten 13l+ in first three runs for Ralph Beckett and this yard and she ran no race at all on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; 50-1) last week; can only watch.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xxx & Chris Rees
Spotlight: Three runs at about 1m on AW, faring slightly better at Wolverhampton in January when holding a prominent position until over 1f out and not persevered with; goes handicapping off a lowly mark in an ordinary race over a trip that should suit; very likely to outstrip early efforts; market will guide.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Some early promise over 7f and 1m on the AW this spring but it's been hard work for him in handicaps on AW and turf, in headgear on last two starts, and he has lots to find now taking a big step up in trip.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Dam bred one who was successful at 1m2f on AW but most of this one's pedigree leans towards shorter trips and she's no good thing to improve on her handful of starts at up to 1m now right up in distance; cheekpieces on.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: In the frame three times since leaving maiden and novice company, including in 1m4f classified on Lingfield AW recently; this slightly shorter trip could help back on Tapeta but more needed; cheekpieces refitted.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
OwnerName: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer
Spotlight: Minor form as yet and her stamina for this far has to be proved following two weak finishes over 1m2f on turf, dropping to a classified event at Nottingham (first-time cheekpieces, retained) on Tuesday.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Little of note in novice and maiden events and seemingly little expected on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good to firm; 25-1) this month when he was never involved; much more needed for new cheekpieces.

RaceName: Get Raceday Ready Handicap
Spotlight: Nothing to latch on to as yet in just four starts and the writing was on the wall a long way out on handicap debut at Windsor (about 1m3f; 66-1) in May; wore cheekpieces that day but they're left off now; others more likely.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Dual bumper and maiden hurdle winner; some decent Flat runs in six attempts including over C&D last time when he faced a difficult task against some quality rivals; can't be ruled out back in a handicap.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice last term over approx' 1m6f at Sligo and the Curragh; didn't build on solid Navan seasonal bow when well beaten last time at the Curragh; down in grade today and can do better.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Hurdle winner has won twice on the Flat, the latest coming over 2m1f at Limerick in 2025; not bad runs lately including when not getting the cleanest of trips at Killarney last time; one to consider.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice at this track last year over 1m4f/1m5f; the best of three runs this term came back here last time over the longer trip; steps up in trip today and a big player if staying; raised 1lb.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden has been poor on last two starts (bled at Fairyhouse first time); opposed with a visor added.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark at Navan over 1m5f and good efforts since over just short of this trip at Bellewstown and Killarney; needs a bit more off this mark.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins up to 2m; below form on the Flat and over hurdles the last twice and isn't easy to fancy at present despite a dropping mark.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Has been poor in five hurdle starts and was well beaten in a Tramore Flat claimer lately so others preferred.

RaceName: Turkish Jockey Club Handicap
Spotlight: Her only Flat win in 33 starts came in this race in 2023 on soft ground; best run this year was on soft at Down Royal; has to improve on latest two runs; ground concerns.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Posted an improved effort when winning on his handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW) two weeks ago, pulling 3l clear with the runner-up; now 5lb higher but he's open to further progress; contender.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice on the AW at Kempton in October (1m/7f) but he's struggled in three starts this year; all starts on turf have been on ground slower than good, and he has a bit to prove.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Solid fourth on his handicap debut at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month and built on that when winning at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) two weeks ago; steps up in grade off this 3lb higher mark but he could have more to offer after just five starts.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark when winning over this C&D in April off 4lb lower; raced too freely over 1m2f at Newmarket in May and he was well held on the July Course last month; return to this track may help.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent 6yo who was beaten under 1l when fourth of eight at Chester (extended 1m4f, good) 12 days ago and will be fine over this shorter trip; likely to be on the premises again.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who was creditable third of 14 under Harry Rimell in stronger race than this at York (extended 1m2f, good) last month; good chance off same mark here, assuming he handles the faster ground.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Went close at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) for Alan King in February but dropped out quite tamely on recent stable debut (2m, AW) and this shorter trip may not be ideal for him nowadays.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Began this year with three respectable 7f AW runs in blinkers (missing today) and can also be effective over 1m2f on turf but has fitness to prove after 145-day break and his last win came in summer 2024.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Belied 50-1 odds when second of 22 under Chelsea Edwards at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in March and put some poor efforts behind him when third at Ffos Las (1m, good; refitted cheekpieces) last month but is not easy to predict and has stamina to prove over this new trip.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Came good at ninth attempt, when back on turf at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) last month; nudged up only 2lb for that and remains unexposed over this trip; big player if aided by new tongue-tie and/or cheekpieces.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Caroline Instone & J S Moore
Spotlight: Has much better strike-rate on AW but has performed well on fast turf here twice in recent weeks (1m3f/1m2f) and is still on a workable mark.

RaceName: Frasers Plus Designer Outlet Swindon Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Gained his last four wins on heavy ground at Ffos Las (1m-1m2f); handles fast ground but is yet to strike form after three runs this season and might still be too high in the weights.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten over 7f on first two starts and may have been flattered by proximity when fifth in slowly run race at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last time; drops in trip again for handicap/AW debut; improvement needed.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Displayed improvement to win in first-time blinkers for William Haggas at Newcastle (6f) in January but subsequent 6f/7f form for two different stables has been disappointing (latest run was her first in a visor); drops to 5f with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Weakened into fifth of six after racing too freely on handicap debut at Catterick (6f, good to firm) in May but remains unexposed and may benefit from this drop in trip.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Dead-heated on handicap debut at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) in May and bounced back from a lesser effort when keeping-on second at Ffos Las (5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; not yet proven on AW surfaces but still respected.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Remains a maiden after 20 starts but was placed in C&D classified last month (wore cheekpieces) and again ran quite well in that grade at Wolverhampton 16 days ago; back in a handicap off career-low mark; has a big role to play.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: 0-6 on turf but did not have an ideal track position when going close at Beverley (5f, good to firm) nine days ago; effectively 1lb higher today but due to be another 1lb higher soon; will be dangerous if suited by this new surface.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to show any significant promise after six runs (6f-7f); dropped in trip and tried in a visor today.
